course_description:
Deepen your understanding of modern biotechnology through real-world case studies spanning medical, agricultural, and industrial sectors. Explore innovation, intellectual property strategy, patents, and the role of AI in biotechnology. Develop critical thinking around ethical, regulatory, and commercial challenges, while building practical skills in science communication and patent navigation through industry engagement and applied workshops. Engage with local biotech leaders to explore emerging innovations driving the global biotechnology landscape.

In this course, you will learn from leading experts in academia and industry as we explore key concepts, challenges, and innovations shaping modern biotechnology. Through real-world case studies and workshops, you will gain a broad understanding of how biotechnology is transforming the medical, agricultural, and industrial sectors. You will also develop practical skills in intellectual property, science communication, and data sourcing, while engaging critically with ethical, social, and commercial issues that influence innovation and translation.

course_aims:
This course aims to present students with:

a) a broad range of core technologies and applications across medical, agricultural, and industrial biotechnology;

b) key concepts in intellectual property, patents, and innovation,

c) emerging innovations and challenges in the local and global biotechnology sectors, and

d) critical thinking about the ethical, social, and commercial issues influencing biotechnology research and product development.

learning_outcomes.description: Explain the relevance of intellectual property concepts in varied biotechnological contexts;
Interrogate patent and scientific databases using tools employed by biotechnology professionals;
Interrogate the underpinning science of core technologies across medical, agricultural, and industrial biotechnology, including their scientific foundations and applications;
Describe the therapeutic drug development pipeline and clinical trials process.

assessment_details.title: Innovation Pitch
assessment_details.due_date: 8/10/2025 2:00 pm Detailed instructions on how to complete this task are provided on Blackboard.
assessment_details.task_description: The ability to learn independently and to communicate effectively are both key traits that are valuable not only in a career, but in most fields. In the 'Innovation Pitch' assessment, students are required to prepare a video recording 5 minutes long, which provides a critical analysis and discussion of a topic pertinent to Biotechnology Innovation. Detailed instructions on how to complete this task are provided on Blackboard. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your Innovation Pitch through the course submission point in Blackboard. Before submitting your assessment item in Blackboard, name your file in the following way: FAMILY NAME_GivenName_StudentID_CourseCode_AssignmentName. For example: Bob Smith (ID: 54329876) would name his ABCD1234 essay 1 in the following way: SMITH_Bob_54329876_ABCD1234_Essay1
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Applications for extension
Extension applications must be received no later than 24 hours after the published assessment due date and time.
Read the information contained in the following links carefully before submitting an application for extension to assessment due date.
For guidance on applying for an extension, information is available here .
For the policy relating to extensions, information is available here (Part D).
Information on medical certificates
Please note the University's requirements for medical certificates here .
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date. For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period). 10% will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point your submission will receive a mark of zero (0) unless an extension has been approved.
In most instances one or more hurdles will apply to your assessment item so you will need to submit it to fulfil the requirements of the course regardless of how late it is and the mark you are likely to be awarded.
